steps.title: Convert area units from square feet to square meters
steps.content:
The conversion factor is given: 1 meter = 3.28 feet. Since we're working with area (square units), we must square the conversion factor:
$$1 \text{ m}^2 = (3.28)^2 \text{ ft}^2 = 10.7584 \text{ ft}^2$$
To convert 310 square feet per hour to square meters per hour, divide by the conversion factor:
$$\frac{310 \text{ ft}^2}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1 \text{ m}^2}{10.7584 \text{ ft}^2} = \frac{310}{10.7584} \approx 28.81 \text{ m}^2/\text{hr}$$

steps.title: Convert time units from per hour to per minute
steps.content:
Now convert from square meters per hour to square meters per minute. Since 1 hour = 60 minutes, divide by 60:
$$\frac{28.81 \text{ m}^2}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1 \text{ hr}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{28.81}{60} \approx 0.4802 \text{ m}^2/\text{min}$$
Rounding to match the precision of the answer choices, this is approximately 0.48 square meters per minute. That matches choice D — D is correct.
